skill.name: next-js-patterns
skill.description: Best practices and patterns for Next.js App Router, Server Actions, and Routing in this project.
skill.summary: Best practices and patterns for Next.js App Router, Server Actions, and Routing in this project.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a documented fetch example, and a weak-crypto pattern on the description line. Review found these are documentation false positives, with no executable commands, external endpoint, cryptographic implementation, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code references and a fenced TSX example. They document Next.js file paths, imports, hooks, fonts, and metadata, but do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every cited location is plain Markdown documentation or a fenced TSX example. No shell execution syntax, script file, or instruction to run external commands appears in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documented Fetch Usage
low_findings.description: The network detection is guidance that Server Components can use fetch or database calls. It does not name an external endpoint, send secrets, or perform a network request from the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only fetch reference is instructional text for Next.js data fetching. There is no URL, payload, credential access, or executable code path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography detection points to the frontmatter description. The line describes Next.js App Router, Server Actions, and routing, with no cryptographic algorithm or implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is natural-language metadata about framework patterns. It contains no hash, cipher, random number generation, or security-sensitive code.

content.value_statement: Teams often mix App Router patterns across pages, layouts, navigation, data loading, fonts, and metadata. This skill gives concise Next.js conventions for consistent project code.
actual_capabilities: Explains where App Router pages and layouts should live.
Clarifies when to use Client Components instead of Server Components.
Recommends Link and useRouter for common navigation flows.
Describes basic Server Component and Client Component data fetching choices.
Notes project conventions for next/font and metadata exports.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not modify project files by itself.
It is focused on Next.js 15 App Router projects.
It does not validate code against a live Next.js build.
It does not cover Pages Router or advanced deployment configuration.

use_cases.title: Create consistent routes
use_cases.description: Use the skill to place pages and layouts in the expected App Router folders.

use_cases.title: Review component boundaries
use_cases.description: Use the skill to decide whether a component should stay on the server or use client behavior.

use_cases.title: Onboard contributors
use_cases.description: Use the skill as a short project reference for navigation, data fetching, fonts, and metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Place a new page
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the Next.js patterns skill, tell me where to add a new dashboard page and what files I should consider.

prompt_templates.title: Choose server or client
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component idea against the skill. Should it be a Server Component or a Client Component?

prompt_templates.title: Plan navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to design internal navigation for this flow, including when Link or useRouter is appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an App Router feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this planned Next.js feature against the skill for routing, layout, data fetching, fonts, and metadata consistency.

output_examples.input: Where should I add a settings page?
output_examples.output: A short recommendation that places the page under the App Router route folder and notes when a nested layout is useful.

output_examples.input: Should my search panel use client behavior?
output_examples.output: A component-boundary recommendation based on state, effects, and browser API needs.
A checklist covering route placement, layout reuse, data fetching choice, font usage, and metadata coverage.

best_practices: Start with Server Components unless the feature needs state, effects, or browser APIs.
Use Link for standard internal navigation and useRouter for programmatic client navigation.
Define metadata near the page or layout that owns the route.
anti_patterns: Adding use client to every component without a browser-side requirement.
Using programmatic navigation when a normal internal link is sufficient.
Scattering font and metadata decisions across unrelated components.

faq.question: Does this skill generate Next.js code?
faq.answer: It can guide code generation, but the skill itself is a concise pattern reference.

faq.question: Does it support the Pages Router?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is written for the Next.js App Router.

faq.question: Can it help with Server Components?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains the default Server Component approach and when Client Components are needed.

faq.question: Does it include data fetching guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers direct server fetching and client fetching with effects or query tools.

faq.question: Does it perform network requests?
faq.answer: No. It only mentions fetch as a Next.js data fetching pattern.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found only documentation false positives and no executable risky behavior.
